title: Clean a Bathroom Sink
summary: Clean and wipe down a bathroom sink and faucet.
learnerPromise: You will be able to clean a bathroom sink and faucet so they look shiny and are free of soap scum and water spots.
whyItMatters: Bathroom sinks collect soap, toothpaste, and water spots daily. Regular cleaning keeps the surface hygienic and makes the whole bathroom look fresher.
realLifeUses: Quick daily wipe-down after brushing teeth
Preparing a bathroom before guests visit
Deep cleaning during a weekly chore routine
Removing buildup from hard water or soap residue
materialsNeeded: All-purpose bathroom cleaner or mild soap
result: A clean, shiny sink basin and faucet with no visible soap scum or water spots.
youWillLearn: How to apply cleaner and let it sit for best results
How to scrub the basin without scratching the surface
How to clean around and behind the faucet
How to polish the faucet and handles for a shiny finish
miniChallenge: Clean the bathroom sink and faucet in your home today. Make the faucet shine.
steps: Remove items from the sink area and rinse the basin with warm water.
Spray or wipe cleaner over the basin, faucet, and handles.
Let the cleaner sit for one to two minutes to loosen buildup.
Scrub the basin and faucet with a cloth or sponge, using an old toothbrush for tight spots.
Rinse thoroughly with warm water and dry with a clean cloth to prevent water spots.
completionCriteria: Sink basin and faucet are visibly clean with no soap scum or spots
Water runs freely and the drain area is wiped clear
commonProblems: Stubborn stains around the drain — scrub with a paste of baking soda and water
Water spots keep appearing — dry the faucet after rinsing, do not let it air dry
Buildup behind the faucet — wrap a damp cloth around the area and let it sit before scrubbing
tips: Clean from top to bottom so dirt falls into the sink and gets rinsed away last
Do not mix different cleaners — use one product at a time
A daily quick wipe takes under a minute and prevents heavy buildup
